title: Best Man For The Gob

jokes.text: Well, the thermostat is in my office. While it's freezing in here, it's still hot in there. So please continue.
jokes.setup: Accountant complains about the cold office temperature
jokes.punchline: Michael acknowledges the temperature control problem but dismisses it to continue business
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's priorities and slight obliviousness to others' comfort

jokes.text: This is like the bayou in here. - I tried to tell you that. I guess I just had to find out for myself.
jokes.setup: Temperature complaints continue with dramatic comparison
jokes.punchline: Michael's need to experience everything himself despite being warned
jokes.explanation: Excellent character trait establishment - Michael's stubborn need to verify everything himself

jokes.text: You're asking Gilligan not to lie? Please call me Ira. I don't like Gilligan. Who doesn't like Gilligan?
jokes.setup: Play on accountant's name Ira Gilligan
jokes.punchline: Gob's confusion about why anyone wouldn't like the beloved TV character
jokes.explanation: Classic AD wordplay with character confusion - sets up ongoing name confusion gag

jokes.text: Yeah, I don't think she'd go for that. Besides, I talked to Dad. He said it was stupid to get married in the first place. And he's right. He's right. I've gotta get rid of her.
jokes.setup: Gob discussing bachelor party plans
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal from celebration to elimination based on father's opinion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob behavior - impulsive and easily swayed by father figure authority

jokes.text: Tell her that I'm insensitive and unreliable. Something about how you can never confront people. You need someone to clean up your messes. See, that's great. And that's just off the top of your head.
jokes.setup: Gob asking Michael to lie about his character flaws
jokes.punchline: Gob praising Michael for accurately describing his real flaws
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Gob completely missing that Michael is describing his actual flaws, not making them up

jokes.text: I don't want to blame it all on 9/11... but it certainly didn't help.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining marital problems
jokes.punchline: Blaming a national tragedy for personal relationship issues
jokes.explanation: Iconic AD absurdism - inappropriate blame attribution that became widely quoted

jokes.text: Oh, no. Oh, no was right.
jokes.setup: Michael's reaction to family band mention
jokes.punchline: Narrator confirming the appropriateness of Michael's dread
jokes.explanation: Classic narrator validation technique - breaks fourth wall slightly

jokes.text: Dr. Fünke's 100% Natural Good-Time Family-Band Solution
jokes.setup: Introduction of family band name
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously long, corporate-sounding band name
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of corporate naming conventions applied to folk music

jokes.text: We keep you alive.
jokes.setup: Description of pharmaceutical company's simple motto
jokes.punchline: Ominously minimal promise for a drug company
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about pharmaceutical companies' low bar for success

jokes.text: I don't know. I probably stole it. I mean, if he says it's missing, it is. Unless he stole it. Hey, maybe he stole it. He's the one that said it's missing. Yeah, I probably stole it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about missing money
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s circular logic ending where it started
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. paranoia and circular thinking - both paranoid and self-incriminating

jokes.text: Gilligan has promised me that all this money will be saved in I.R.A.s. - It's Ira, sir. - Oh, I'm sorry, Gilligan. Will be saved in 'Iras.'
jokes.setup: Confusion between IRA accounts and accountant's name
jokes.punchline: George making the financial term personal by changing it to 'Iras'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay building on established name confusion - financial acronym becomes personal

jokes.text: I'm fun. It's just that... Listen. The way to fix this Ira thing is not to buy him off. All right? It's to find out where the money went. You're no fun. Yeah. I'm plenty fun.
jokes.setup: Michael taking the ethical approach instead of the fun approach
jokes.punchline: Michael's defensive insistence that he's fun while being completely unfun
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Michael's insecurity about being perceived as boring

jokes.text: I have always wanted a daughter. And a blonde. What fun.
jokes.setup: Lucille welcoming Gob's new wife
jokes.punchline: Reducing daughter-in-law to hair color preference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille superficiality - treating people like accessories

jokes.text: Those are for his soccer team. No sugar for you. You just get more awful.
jokes.setup: Annyong wanting juice boxes
jokes.punchline: Lucille's casual cruelty toward adopted child
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille meanness - treating child like a pest

jokes.text: Hey, Adopted Brother. Do you think I could have a hit of that juice box?
jokes.setup: Buster addressing Annyong
jokes.punchline: Using 'Adopted Brother' as formal title and treating juice like drugs
jokes.explanation: Great Buster childishness - treating juice like contraband and formal family titles

jokes.text: For 15 minutes. Then it burns when you pee, and your marriage goes to hell.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Zanotab's effects
jokes.punchline: Dramatic escalation from minor side effect to life destruction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from physical discomfort to relationship destruction

jokes.text: Some call me the human metronome. You notice how I'm always on time? I'm never late for things. Yeah, but I think punctuality is slightly different from rhythm. No, it's not. It's the exact same thing. It's knowing how long things take.
jokes.setup: Michael offering to play wood block
jokes.punchline: Conflating punctuality with musical rhythm through flawed logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - applying business mindset to creative endeavors with confidence

jokes.text: We've been talking here for 28 seconds. Just so you know.
jokes.setup: Michael demonstrating his time awareness
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately precise timing of casual conversation
jokes.explanation: Great callback demonstrating Michael's obsessive time consciousness

jokes.text: It was like you knew every side effect I was going through at that time. Sadly, Teamocil has been discontinued. The sense of wellness it created in relationships was merely the first sign of complete pituitary shutdown.
jokes.setup: Fan praising Tobias's pharmaceutical song
jokes.punchline: Tobias casually mentioning the drug caused organ failure
jokes.explanation: Dark pharmaceutical humor - wellness leading to organ shutdown delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I smell olives!
jokes.setup: Normal conversation about Michael's voice
jokes.punchline: Random olive observation interrupting serious moment
jokes.explanation: Random non-sequitur that establishes character's odd priorities

jokes.text: Are you hitting on me? Gob said that would happen.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to subtly criticize Gob
jokes.punchline: Wife recognizing Michael's pattern because Gob warned her
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - Michael's plan backfires because Gob knows his patterns

jokes.text: You've got her hating me... at least your version of me, which is actually you.
jokes.setup: Michael confronting Gob about wife's opinion
jokes.punchline: Michael realizing Gob described himself while claiming to describe Michael
jokes.explanation: Clever psychological insight - people project their own flaws when describing others

jokes.text: It's like, so far it's been all chain and no ball.
jokes.setup: Gob describing marriage disappointments
jokes.punchline: Sexual wordplay on 'ball and chain' marriage metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of common marriage metaphor with sexual implication

jokes.text: No, no. Your bachelor party's about convincing Gilligan that he's killed a stripper... getting him out of town so he can't testify... and making sure that people think that he took the money.
jokes.setup: Gob thinking bachelor party is about fun
jokes.punchline: George's elaborate criminal scheme disguised as party planning
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. criminal thinking - elaborate schemes presented as simple plans

jokes.text: That's why I want you to use this stripper... a narcoleptic, believe it or not.
jokes.setup: George explaining his scheme
jokes.punchline: Specific medical condition as criminal tool
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd criminal planning - exploiting medical condition for fraud

jokes.text: We wanted you to have the first piece!
jokes.setup: Stripper jumping out of cake at bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Innocent cake-cutting phrase in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate context humor - innocent phrase in sexual situation

jokes.text: We are pushers, not takers.
jokes.setup: Tobias refusing to let family use supplements
jokes.punchline: Drug dealer terminology for pharmaceutical sales
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - applying drug dealer ethics to pharmaceutical promotion

jokes.text: We are pushers, not takers.
jokes.setup: Discussing family drug use policy
jokes.punchline: Using drug dealer terminology for pharmaceutical promotion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about the family's pharmaceutical promotion business

jokes.text: Let's take it from loose stool!
jokes.setup: Tobias directing band practice
jokes.punchline: Medical side effect as musical cue
jokes.explanation: Great cringe humor - treating pharmaceutical side effects as song sections

jokes.text: Already down! Get your finger out of my throat! It's down!
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to make Lindsay spit out pill
jokes.punchline: Physical struggle over pharmaceutical consumption
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - domestic dispute over drug use

jokes.text: We get to stay at a hotel, get up at 4:00 a.m., slap on the old seasick patch. We're gonna get out there on the choppy ocean. We're gonna catch ourselves a little lunch. Oh, we get to eat it? After we gut it.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sell fishing as fun
jokes.punchline: Each detail making the trip sound progressively worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of unpleasant details presented as positives

jokes.text: Because Buster's your brother... and I'm not gonna leave him home alone with all this J-U-I-C-E around. I can spell, Mom. You spelled 'juice.' Oh, you're so brilliant. Let's see you find it.
jokes.setup: Lucille spelling out 'juice' to hide it from Buster
jokes.punchline: Buster recognizing the spelling but Lucille treating it like a scavenger hunt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille-Buster dynamic - treating adult son like small child

jokes.text: Real fun. Okay? Not everything is strippers and booze and buckets of blood. Why do you guys have buckets of blood?
jokes.setup: Michael dismissing bachelor party as low-class fun
jokes.punchline: Michael's delayed realization of the suspicious blood reference
jokes.explanation: Good delayed reaction - Michael's moral high ground undermined by obvious red flag

jokes.text: Not everything is strippers and booze and buckets of blood.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his fishing plans
jokes.punchline: Listing increasingly specific and disturbing party elements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's inadvertent knowledge of the party plans

jokes.text: We have unlimited juice? This party is gonna be off the hook.
jokes.setup: Brothers discussing corn syrup and red dye
jokes.punchline: Buster thinking it's unlimited juice access
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - innocent interpretation of sinister materials

jokes.text: We should be dressing like it's the '80s.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about '60s costumes
jokes.punchline: Suggesting '80s costumes would be more futuristic in 21st century
jokes.explanation: Mild fashion confusion - Lindsay's warped sense of what's contemporary

jokes.text: I'm tired of trying to find happiness through lies and self-medicating. If you need me, I'll be at the bar.
jokes.setup: Lindsay declaring she's done with unhealthy coping mechanisms
jokes.punchline: Immediately going to engage in drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction - rejecting self-medication while heading to bar

jokes.text: You think you can handle it alone? 'Cause if you don't perform, we don't validate.
jokes.setup: Maeby abandoning father for solo performance
jokes.punchline: Threatening financial consequences for poor performance
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - teenager threatening parent with business consequences

jokes.text: Oh, they think they're having fun, Son, but it's not lasting fun. It is fleeting fun, like a bachelor party. What we got here is lasting fun.
jokes.setup: Michael hearing party sounds from hotel room
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate rationalization of boring fishing trip as superior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-justification - intellectual superiority to mask insecurity

jokes.text: I have a pretty finely tuned internal clock... which is why I'm actually such a good natural percussionist.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining why he can't sleep
jokes.punchline: Referencing Michael's earlier metronome claim
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing genetic trait inheritance through flawed logic

jokes.text: Wait a minute. 'Too proud?' What does this have to do with 'too proud?' Actually, that part was kinda just for you. I was just worried the whole thing wasn't gonna land unless I included the pride part.
jokes.setup: George Michael manipulating Michael into letting him go to party
jokes.punchline: George Michael admitting to manipulating Michael's psychology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment - character explaining their own manipulation tactics

jokes.text: You told me to hire people to look like my friends. They're Hot Cops. They're entertainers. Although I specifically asked them not to come dressed as cops.
jokes.setup: George Sr. asking about cops at bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Hot Cops entertainment service ignoring costume requests
jokes.explanation: Standard AD absurdism - professional entertainers with confusing rules

jokes.text: Hey! What the hell happened to the blood? Hey, Brother. Whoa! Somebody put on his lipstick in the car. I love juice!
jokes.setup: Missing fake blood for scheme
jokes.punchline: Buster covered in 'blood' from drinking corn syrup mixture
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy - Buster's addiction ruining criminal scheme

jokes.text: Teamocil! You're out of the band.
jokes.setup: Fan shouting pharmaceutical name during performance
jokes.punchline: Tobias firing audience member for engagement
jokes.explanation: Good performer-audience interaction gone wrong

jokes.text: Unfortunately, he was unaware of the narcoleptic stripper confined within it.
jokes.setup: Buster finding cake to satisfy sugar craving
jokes.punchline: Cake contains unconscious person
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual setup for upcoming chaos

jokes.text: She died. She's dead. It was Gilligan! Gilligan killed the skipper... stripper.
jokes.setup: Fake death scheme revelation
jokes.punchline: Gob mixing up 'Gilligan's Island' reference with plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback combining name confusion with TV show reference

jokes.text: Not again.
jokes.setup: Stripper waking up to see bloody Buster
jokes.punchline: Implying this has happened before
jokes.explanation: Dark humor suggesting repeated violent encounters in her profession

jokes.text: What does it matter? I screwed it up anyway. A complete failure. Can't even fake the death of a stripper.
jokes.setup: Gob's self-pity after the scheme fails
jokes.punchline: Treating failure to commit fake murder as a personal inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob - measuring his self-worth by ability to execute criminal schemes

jokes.text: I might be a father.
jokes.setup: Michael saying Gob might not be a father but is his brother
jokes.punchline: Gob's delayed realization of potential parenthood
jokes.explanation: Good delayed realization moment typical of Gob's scattered thinking

jokes.text: I might be a father.
jokes.setup: Michael saying Gob might not be a father but is his brother
jokes.punchline: Gob's casual admission of potential paternity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob delivery of shocking personal information. The casual tone makes it funnier and shows his emotional avoidance.

jokes.text: I'm in love with your brother. What? You did it again, you son of a bitch! No, no! She's... In-law. Tobias.
jokes.setup: Wife declaring love for Michael
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she means brother-in-law Tobias
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with family relationship clarification

jokes.text: Oh, my god. I killed Michael. Yeah, but on the plus side, you got punched in the face.
jokes.setup: Gob's shock at attacking Michael
jokes.punchline: George Sr. finding silver lining in family violence
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor - finding positives in dysfunction

jokes.text: Let it ring.
jokes.setup: Phone ringing with Gilligan's Island theme
jokes.punchline: Ira's resigned acceptance of the musical torture
jokes.explanation: Final callback to Gilligan theme with character's defeated acceptance

tmdb_overview: George Sr. uses Gob's bachelor party as an opportunity to set up his accountant in a compromising position so that he can blackmail him.  Meanwhile, Michael tries to prove he's a fun guy, and the Funkes reunite their family folk-music band.
insight: Gob's wedding spirals into 53 jokes at 1.95-per-minute velocity, anchored by character chaos and escalating absurdity.
